heroHeadline: US-Iran Ceasefire Opens Window for World Cup Participation — 64 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: A US-Iran ceasefire announced April 8, mediated by Pakistan, opens the first genuine diplomatic path for Iran's World Cup participation — which Iran's Sports Minister had rated 'very low' under active hostilities. The ceasefire directly satisfies Iran's stated precondition that 'security guarantees be provided' before reconsideration, but Iran's government has not yet issued a revised statement on World Cup attendance. The FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30 — now 22 days away — remains the critical formal decision point. Meanwhile, USMNT striker Patrick Agyemang has been officially ruled out of the tournament after MRI confirmed a ruptured Achilles tendon, dealing a further blow to Pochettino's attacking options with 64 days until the opener against Paraguay. FIFA's venue inspection tour begins April 14 across all 16 host cities.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN
